comments.body:
/hold

need to check why we get a missing podman during build

https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5418525380837376

@edsantiago when you have a moment, could you take a quick peek?

We were having loads of problems today with CI jobs failing 15+ minutes in, because of one networking issue or another.  This PR isn't a perfect check, but hopefully in the common-case (like unreachable docker.io) we don't waste time and resources on further testing.  It also (maybe) helps make it obvious there's a networking problem, rather than having to troll through the test-logs.

Isn't this a wee bit more complicated than it needs to be? `netcat` is already installed on all the systems, isn't it?
```bash
while read host port; do nc -zv -w 10 $host $port || die; done < LIST-OF-HOST-PORTS
```
No need for containers, Dockerfiles, complicated scripts. (For future such endeavors, though, could you give scripts a more descriptive name than `entrypoint`?)

> Isn't this a wee bit more complicated than it needs to be?

Likely :grinning: 

> netcat is already installed on all the systems, isn't it?

We need to use a container image since they start and run so much faster than VMs.  Not sure about netcat, though it's a great suggestion.  I can check into doing it that way, perhaps with an image we already build.

Thanks!
No `netcat` in either Fedora nor Centos container images.  Will check our existing gate and imgts images when I have a better network link myself (I'm on the bus).

alpine has it:
```
# podman run alpine nc -z -v -w 10 l.edsantiago.com 800
nc: l.edsantiago.com (72.14.191.63:800): Operation timed out
```

A hack I do, is to execute on a Fedora box

podman run -v /usr/bin/nc:/usr/bin/nc fedora  nc -z -v -w 10 l.edsantiago.com 800

Then you don't need to do anything special in the container, one problem with this is if the containers libraries are different then the hosts.
Checked, and found our gate container has it, so I just added a new section with @edsantiago magic loop.

Yay, thanks @edsantiago 

```
while read host port; do nc -zv -w 13 $host $port || exit 1; done < ${CIRRUS_WORKING_DIR}/${SCRIPT_BASE}/required_host_ports.txt
Ncat: Version 7.70 ( https://nmap.org/ncat )
Ncat: Connected to 192.30.253.113:22.
Ncat: 0 bytes sent, 0 bytes received in 0.04 seconds.
Ncat: Version 7.70 ( https://nmap.org/ncat )
Ncat: Connected to 52.54.178.62:443.
Ncat: 0 bytes sent, 0 bytes received in 0.06 seconds.
Ncat: Version 7.70 ( https://nmap.org/ncat )
Ncat: Connected to 23.21.165.246:443.
Ncat: 0 bytes sent, 0 bytes received in 0.04 seconds.
Ncat: Version 7.70 ( https://nmap.org/ncat )

Ncat: 0 bytes sent, 0 bytes received in 0.13 seconds.
```
